skill.name: waapi
skill.description: Guidance for building deterministic Web Animations API motion in HyperFrames compositions.
skill.summary: Author seekable native browser animations for HyperFrames.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URL patterns. Review found a documentation-only skill with WAAPI examples, benign validation commands, and MDN reference links; no malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Shell-Execution False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static Ruby shell execution matches are markdown inline code and fenced examples for Web Animations API usage. The only shell commands are validation commands at lines 85-87, with fixed arguments and no user input interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is markdown documentation, and the matched backticks are code formatting. The validation commands are explicit fixed commands for the user to run manually.

low_findings.title: Benign Documentation URL References
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to MDN Web Animations API documentation. No code performs network requests or sends local data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a credits and references section. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, telemetry, or exfiltration behavior in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography matches occur in a description of Web Animations API usage, not cryptographic code. The reconnaissance matches are ordinary animation guidance and section text, not host inspection or system enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain animation terminology and documentation headings. I found no cryptographic operations, filesystem probing, environment probing, or system command enumeration.

content.value_statement: Native browser animations can drift when timelines are not seekable. This skill shows how to author Web Animations API motion that HyperFrames can control deterministically.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use the HyperFrames WAAPI runtime adapter.
Shows how to create animations synchronously during composition initialization.
Demonstrates finite duration, delay, easing, fill mode, and iteration settings.
Documents how document-level animation time maps to HyperFrames seeking.
Provides validation commands for checking a HyperFrames composition.
limitations: Does not include the HyperFrames WAAPI adapter implementation.
Does not cover complex animation libraries such as GSAP.
Assumes the composition runs in a browser with Web Animations API support.
Requires manual validation after adapting animation code.

use_cases.title: Create seekable DOM motion
use_cases.description: Use native keyframes for transform and opacity changes that must render consistently at any timeline position.

use_cases.title: Replace simple library timelines
use_cases.description: Use WAAPI for lightweight animation sequences when a full animation library is unnecessary.

use_cases.title: Generate animations from structured data
use_cases.description: Convert timing data into finite WAAPI animations that HyperFrames can pause and seek.

prompt_templates.title: Start a WAAPI animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple HyperFrames WAAPI animation for one element. Use finite timing, fill both, and pause the animation after creation.

prompt_templates.title: Convert CSS motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert my CSS keyframe motion into a Web Animations API pattern that HyperFrames can seek deterministically.

prompt_templates.title: Design a staggered sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a staggered WAAPI sequence for several elements. Use delays for offsets and avoid render-critical promises or callbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit timeline determinism
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this HyperFrames animation plan for deterministic seeking. Identify any use of infinite iterations, timers, layout animation, or clip offset mistakes.

output_examples.input: I need a title card to slide in and remain visible while the frame is seeked.
output_examples.output: Use a finite WAAPI animation with transform and opacity keyframes. Set fill mode to both, pause after creation, and model the start offset with delay.

output_examples.input: My tokens animate with a timer and look different after seeking.
output_examples.output: Replace timer-driven updates with finite animations created during initialization. Use staggered delays and let HyperFrames control current animation time.

output_examples.input: I want to validate a composition after editing WAAPI motion.
output_examples.output: Run the HyperFrames lint and validation checks after editing. Review any timing, fill mode, or unsupported animation warnings before rendering.

best_practices: Create WAAPI animations synchronously during composition initialization.
Use finite durations, finite iterations, and fill mode both for seekable states.
Represent clip offsets with animation delay or HyperFrames-controlled visibility.
anti_patterns: Do not depend on animation.finished for render-critical DOM changes.
Do not run independent clocks with timers, requestAnimationFrame, or performance.now.
Do not animate layout properties when transform and opacity can express the motion.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps create Web Animations API motion that HyperFrames can pause, seek, and render consistently.

faq.question: Does this skill install an animation library?
faq.answer: No. It documents native browser Web Animations API patterns and does not add dependencies.

faq.question: Why should animations use finite iterations?
faq.answer: Finite iterations make timeline state predictable when HyperFrames seeks to an exact time.

faq.question: Why use fill mode both?
faq.answer: Fill mode both keeps the expected visual state before and after the active animation interval.

faq.question: Can I use callbacks for important render state?
faq.answer: Avoid callbacks for render-critical state because they may not replay reliably during timeline seeking.

faq.question: How should I check my changes?
faq.answer: Run the HyperFrames lint and validation commands after editing the composition.
